title: Crispy Floral Uruguayan Cornflour Fritters
description: A playful, kid‑friendly fritter that bursts with floral flavor and smoky crunch, inspired by Uruguayan street food.
instructions: In a medium bowl combine corn flour, smoked paprika, thyme, salt, and optional red pepper.
Add water gradually while whisking until a thick, pourable batter forms.
Stir in fresh basil and olive oil until evenly mixed.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat and lightly grease with a small amount of oil.
Drop spoonfuls of batter into the skillet, forming small circles about 2 inches wide.
Cook each fritter for 3 minutes on one side until golden brown and smoke begins to rise.
Flip and cook the other side for an additional 2 minutes until crisp.
Transfer finished fritters to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil and serve hot.
